metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4586
metadata.comment:
Rationale: - Arginine methylation is a major PTM that regulates chromatin structure, transcription, and splicing within the nucleus.

- Figure 1G: Western blot shows upregulated protein arginine methyltransferase PRMT8 expression in the hippocampus when dendritic spine maturation occurs.
- Figure 2: Depletion of PRMT8 by shRNA knockdown leads to overabundance of filopodia and mislocalization of excitatory synapses.
- Figure 4B-D: spine morphology alterations can also be found in hippocampi of PTM8 KO mice.
- Figure 5-6 : Spinning disc, confocal microscopy, FRAP assays and Western blots show that PRMT8 promotes dendritic spine morphology through methylation of the dendritic RNA-binding protein G3BP1 and suppression of the Rac1-PAK1 signaling pathway to control synaptic actin dynamics.. Experimental description: Mouse and rat species are both used throughout figures related to this annotation, we annotate against mouse species based on Figures 4-6 which are central to this annotation (but the same annotation would apply to the respective rat protein).

- Primary hippocampal neurons were cultured from Prmt8 knockout mice Prmt8tm1a(EUCOMM)
- Antibody:Rabbit polyclonal anti-PRMT8 (ICC) Thermo Fisher Scientific (discontinued) Cat#PA5-19846; RRID: AB_10985902
- For making FLAG-tagged PRMT8, the PRMT8 insert was amplified from synthetic full length human PRMT8, the PCR product was digested by EcoRI and KpnI and sub-cloned into the pEGFP-N1 vector.
